[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear after Erdoğan's win. While I voted for him, I worry about the concentration of power and what it means for our future. It's hard to feel hopeful when the opposition is so weak.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a lot of anger and fear right now with the lira crashing. It's hard to see how Erdoğan can frame this as an economic war when our purchasing power is disappearing. I voted for him, but I'm worried about how this will affect our lives.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm feeling a lot of anger and frustration with the government's refusal to comply with the ECHR ruling on Demirtaş. It feels like our institutions are failing us, and I'm worried about the implications for democracy in Turkey.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling frustrated and angry about the recent local election losses for AKP in Ankara and Istanbul. It feels like the government is losing touch with the people, and I'm worried about the direction we're heading.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated and angry about the recent election outcomes. Losing Istanbul and Ankara has shaken my faith in the AKP, and I worry about the future direction of our country.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has really shaken me. It feels like the government is failing to protect women, and I can't help but feel frustrated and angry about the state of our country. This issue is becoming a rallying point for many, and I worry about the implications for our future.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic is really worrying me; I'm anxious about the economic impact and how little support we are getting. I feel frustrated with the government's handling of this crisis, especially since they seem more focused on maintaining power than on helping people like me.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It seems like they are prioritizing their own agenda over the safety and rights of women, which is deeply concerning to me.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration about the government's actions against the HDP. It feels like an attack on democracy and the rights of people who don't share the same views as the ruling party.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling increasingly disillusioned with the government's economic management. The lira's devaluation is hitting us hard, and it's hard to see a way forward. I worry about what this means for my family's future.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling increasingly angry and disillusioned by the government's failure to support pensioners, especially as the minimum wage rises while many retirees remain below that level. It's hard to see a future where my family and I can thrive when those who have worked their whole lives are being neglected.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling really frustrated and angry about the government's failure to address the needs of retirees while inflation is skyrocketing. It's hard to see how we can get by when the cost of living is so high, and the government seems to be ignoring our struggles.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear after Erdoğan's win. While I voted for him, I worry about the concentration of power and what it means for our future. It's hard to feel hopeful when the opposition is so weak.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a lot of anger and fear right now with the lira crashing. It's hard to see how Erdoğan can frame this as an economic war when our purchasing power is disappearing. I voted for him, but I'm worried about how this will affect our lives.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm feeling a lot of anger and frustration with the government's refusal to comply with the ECHR ruling on Demirtaş. It feels like our institutions are failing us, and I'm worried about the implications for democracy in Turkey.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling frustrated and angry about the recent local election losses for AKP in Ankara and Istanbul. It feels like the government is losing touch with the people, and I'm worried about the direction we're heading.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated and angry about the recent election outcomes. Losing Istanbul and Ankara has shaken my faith in the AKP, and I worry about the future direction of our country.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has really shaken me. It feels like the government is failing to protect women, and I can't help but feel frustrated and angry about the state of our country. This issue is becoming a rallying point for many, and I worry about the implications for our future.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic is really worrying me; I'm anxious about the economic impact and how little support we are getting. I feel frustrated with the government's handling of this crisis, especially since they seem more focused on maintaining power than on helping people like me.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It seems like they are prioritizing their own agenda over the safety and rights of women, which is deeply concerning to me.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration about the government's actions against the HDP. It feels like an attack on democracy and the rights of people who don't share the same views as the ruling party.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling increasingly disillusioned with the government's economic management. The lira's devaluation is hitting us hard, and it's hard to see a way forward. I worry about what this means for my family's future.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling increasingly angry and disillusioned by the government's failure to support pensioners, especially as the minimum wage rises while many retirees remain below that level. It's hard to see a future where my family and I can thrive when those who have worked their whole lives are being neglected.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling really frustrated and angry about the government's failure to address the needs of retirees while inflation is skyrocketing. It's hard to see how we can get by when the cost of living is so high, and the government seems to be ignoring our struggles.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the government's handling of the economy and the recent sentencing of İmamoğlu. It seems like the issues affecting everyday people are being ignored.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration about the government's recent actions, especially with the economy. While the retirement elimination seems to help some, it feels like a temporary fix and a way to buy votes. I still have my doubts about whether this will truly benefit us in the long run.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I feel increasingly frustrated with the government's actions, especially the freezing of HDP's treasury aid, which seems politically motivated. This decision only deepens my distrust in the institutions and makes me question the fairness of the upcoming elections.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I'm feeling overwhelmed and scared after the earthquake, and the government's response so far seems lacking. My trust in the institutions is low, and I'm worried about how this will affect our community.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm feeling overwhelmed and angry about the government's response to the earthquake. It's heartbreaking to see so many people suffering and not getting the help they need. My trust in the institutions is at an all-time low.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear about the government's response to the earthquake. Banning Twitter and TikTok during this critical time is unacceptable and shows a lack of concern for the people's needs. My trust in the government is dwindling.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after Erdoğan's admission of problems in the earthquake response. Despite his promises, the government's handling of the situation has left many of us feeling abandoned and fearful for the future.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration due to the government's handling of the construction safety issues that led to the earthquake devastation. The arrest of contractors linked to the scandal only highlights the failures of the AKP and the amnesty policies that allowed this to happen.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling frustrated and angry about how the government has handled the earthquake response. The opposition seems to be stepping up where the AKP has failed, which makes me question my loyalty.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling frustrated with the government's response to the earthquake, especially as I see the opposition mayors taking action. It's making me question my loyalty to the AKP.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling frustrated with the opposition's disunity, especially with Akşener walking out. It's making me question if they can really challenge Erdoğan effectively. The government's response to the earthquake is still weighing heavily on my mind.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and concern about the opposition's ability to unite effectively. While Kılıçdaroğlu's nomination is a step forward, I still question whether they can truly challenge Erdoğan given their past disarray.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and concern about the opposition's ability to unite effectively. The lifting of the HDP treasury aid freeze might help them, but I still doubt if they can truly challenge Erdoğan given their past disarray.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and skepticism about the government's pension increase. While it might help some, the reality is that inflation is so high that it hardly makes a difference for many of us.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and skepticism about the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. It feels like a political maneuver that could further divide us, and I'm worried about how this will affect the election.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling quite skeptical about Erdoğan's last-minute salary raise.
